title: Simulated Dynamics 365

liveOverlay.label: GitHub raw user data from the public repo

frames.label: Frame 01 / Lead Captured
frames.clock: Inbound demo request landed and crossed the routing threshold.
frames.summary: The machine detects a new enterprise lead, opens the qualification queue, and records the first usable customer state.
frames.note: This is the first durable frame: the lead now exists, the routing logic has fired, and the next operator can act without rediscovering context.

transitions.note: Website form created the first CRM record.
The machine confirmed this is net-new demand.

lineage.name: Lead intake snapshot
lineage.note: The inbound lead rows are small enough to materialize directly inside the frame.

lineage.name: Firmographic enrichment
lineage.note: Large enrichment tables stay external and are resolved only when the projection needs them.

lineage.name: Routing score
lineage.note: The score is computed from referenced evidence rather than copied as raw source data.

automations.action: Assign SDR and wake qualification queue

frames.label: Frame 02 / Lead Qualified
frames.clock: Discovery call completed and budget authority was confirmed.
frames.summary: The system upgrades a lead into an account and opportunity, carrying forward all prior context instead of starting from zero.
frames.note: The important move is continuity: the machine is not just storing activity, it is promoting state in a traceable way.

transitions.note: The machine now has a canonical customer record.
Pipeline state became visible and forecastable.
Lead state was preserved instead of discarded.

lineage.name: Canonical CRM promotion
lineage.note: The promoted account and opportunity are stored directly because they define the next machine state.

lineage.name: Transcript archive
lineage.note: The full discovery transcript is too large to copy into every frame, so the frame carries a pointer instead.

lineage.name: Budget confidence
lineage.note: Commercial confidence is recomputed from referenced evidence and the previous frame.

frames.label: Frame 03 / Solution Design
frames.clock: Workshop outputs were approved and the machine moved the deal into structured solutioning.
frames.summary: The system now coordinates cross-team design work, pricing, and implementation planning as a single visible state machine.
frames.note: This is where the CRM stops being a contact log and starts acting like an operational control surface.

transitions.note: Commercial state advanced after technical validation.
Service and success teams were pulled into the same frame.
The machine started preparing proposal outputs.

lineage.name: Solutioning workset
lineage.note: The active commercial records stay materialized so the control surface remains fast to inspect.

lineage.name: Migration inventory
lineage.note: Large asset inventories remain referenced until a service operator drills into them.

lineage.name: Implementation risk rollup
lineage.source: derive(risk <- inventory + workshop findings + case history)
lineage.note: Implementation risk is summarized from linked data rather than copied wholesale.

frames.label: Frame 04 / Quote Approved
frames.clock: Pricing approval and scope review completed, allowing the machine to publish a commercial offer.
frames.summary: The CRM now behaves like a contract machine: quote, approvals, downstream tasks, and risk surfaces all live in one replayable frame.
frames.note: This is where a static site starts to feel like Dynamics 365: forecast, approvals, and execution planning are all synchronized.

transitions.note: The quote can now leave internal review.
Legal and procurement work is now machine-triggered.

lineage.name: Proposal shell
lineage.note: The quote state itself is small and belongs directly in the frame.

lineage.name: Pricing workbook
lineage.note: Detailed pricing schedules stay in the finance dataset and are referenced from the frame.

lineage.name: Margin outlook
lineage.note: Margin is computed from referenced finance data plus the live service scope.

frames.label: Frame 05 / Deal Won, Service Risk Opened
frames.clock: The contract closed, but implementation telemetry detected a migration failure and opened a high-severity case.
frames.summary: This is the useful part of the proof: revenue, onboarding, and incident response all coexist in one machine instead of living in disconnected tools.
frames.note: A real business system has to carry both success and failure at the same time. The frame machine can do that visibly.

transitions.note: Revenue is now recognized as committed business.
Implementation trouble became part of the same visible state.
The lifecycle advanced even as delivery risk appeared.

lineage.name: Customer state snapshot
lineage.note: The new customer and case rows are materialized directly because they are now canonical state.

lineage.name: Migration failure telemetry
lineage.note: Raw failure logs are far too large for the frame, so the page carries a pointer to the telemetry set.

lineage.name: Account health score
lineage.source: derive(health <- telemetry + sponsor activity + onboarding tasks)
lineage.note: Account health is a rollup computed from external telemetry and current frame state.

automations.action: Wake SLA watch and executive alert

frames.label: Frame 06 / Recovery and Renewal
frames.clock: The incident was resolved, account health recovered, and the machine opened the renewal path from the same lineage.
frames.summary: The proof closes the loop: the state machine absorbed delivery trouble, resolved it, and turned the same account into a renewal motion without breaking continuity.
frames.note: This is why the frame model is useful. One visible system can hold demand, revenue, service risk, and future expansion in a single replayable history.

transitions.note: Service state recovered without losing the incident history.
Customer health improved after the intervention landed.
The machine opened the next commercial motion from the same account state.

lineage.name: Renewal state snapshot
lineage.note: The active renewal opportunity is part of the new canonical frame and stays materialized.

lineage.name: Adoption telemetry window
lineage.note: The full product usage stream stays external and is resolved only when operators need a deeper drill-down.

lineage.name: Renewal readiness
lineage.source: derive(readiness <- adoption telemetry + billing health + resolved case history)
lineage.note: The renewal motion is opened from a derived readiness signal, not from a copied raw dataset.

deck: The medium is the message: a static repo, raw JSON state, and a thin runtime are enough to project a portable CRM and service machine anywhere files can be downloaded.

intro.callout: The machine is just files. Frames, overlays, liquid dimensions, and forked variants stay globally accessible because they are published as raw state.
points: Each frame is a serialized system state.
The runtime replays the machine in frame time on a client-side clock.
GitHub raw data acts like a globally accessible state surface.
The checked-in cache keeps a local running-state simulation available.
Forks can inject liquid data as an alternative dimension without abandoning the original state machine.
Any system that can download files can ingest this machine.

portabilityNote: The medium is the message: every visible surface in this repo is also a downloadable state artifact.

relatedProofs.title: Lockstep Digital Twin Console
relatedProofs.note: Twin projection, live adapter response, drift ledger, and correction-frame generation.

importExport.title: Fork backup and reimport
importExport.note: Export the current machine, overlay, and liquid dimension into one bundle, edit it locally, and import it back like nothing happened.
